nodes.task:
Edit templates/workflows/daily-mutator.yaml ONLY. Yaml-only change, no new tests.

Replace the existing single-agent `mutate` step (currently `preset: cc-opus`, `worktree: true`, with the GEPA-mutator task body) with a `type: parallel` step that fans out to one child per role-category. Children must NOT stomp each other's artifacts or gates.

Required shape for the new step (preserve `id: mutate`, `on_complete: eval`, `on_fail: abort`):

  - id: mutate
    type: parallel
    n: 6
    presets: [cc-mutator, cc-mutator, cc-mutator, cc-mutator, cc-mutator, cc-mutator]
    step:
      id: mutate-child
      preset: cc-mutator
      worktree: true
      task: |
        You are a per-domain GEPA mutator. Map your $FLT_RUN_LABEL to one role file:
          a -> templates/roles/coder.md
          b -> templates/roles/reviewer.md
          c -> templates/roles/architect.md
          d -> templates/roles/oracle.md
          e -> templates/roles/mutator.md
          f -> templates/roles/evaluator.md
        If your label is anything else, flt workflow fail "unmapped label: $FLT_RUN_LABEL".

        Inputs:
        - $FLT_RUN_DIR/artifacts/trace-bundle-redacted.txt
        - $FLT_RUN_DIR/artifacts/skill-candidates.txt
        - Focus area: {task}

        Required outputs in $FLT_RUN_DIR/artifacts/ (label-scoped — children must not collide):

        1) <role>-$FLT_RUN_LABEL.vNext.md with YAML front-matter:
        ---
        stable_path: <ABSOLUTE path of the role file you are mutating>
        mutation_type: prompt_edit | skill_acquisition
        ---
        Then the full proposed file body.

        2) <role>-$FLT_RUN_LABEL.hypothesis.json with shape:
        { "artifact": "<relative path>", "mutation_type": "...", "hypothesis": "...", "expected_delta": { "<metric>": "+/-N%" } }

        Rules:
        - Produce exactly one candidate per child.
        - stable_path must be absolute.
        - Do not edit the stable file in place.

        flt workflow pass when both files exist with the label-scoped names.
        flt workflow fail "<one-line reason>" only if you cannot mutate at all.
    on_complete: eval
    on_fail: abort

Keep `collect`, `redact`, `find_skills`, `eval`, and `gate` steps unchanged. Do NOT edit any other yaml file or any source file in this node — auto_pr and other concerns belong to a separate node and conflicts will be reconciled later.

Validate by running:
  bun run typecheck
  bun test

Both must be green. The yaml change is parsed by src/workflow/parser.ts — if you've structured the parallel step correctly, existing parser tests will continue to pass.

flt workflow pass when tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.

Add an `auto_pr: boolean` (default true) field to the workflow yaml schema and gate auto-PR creation on it. TDD: write the failing parser + engine tests first, then implement.

Files to modify:
- src/workflow/types.ts — add `auto_pr?: boolean` to the `WorkflowDef` interface.
- src/workflow/parser.ts — in `validateWorkflowDef`, accept top-level `auto_pr` (must be boolean if present; throw on other types). Return it on the returned object.
- src/workflow/engine.ts — `applyAutoCommit` (around line 553-596) currently always pushes the branch and runs `gh pr create`. Load the WorkflowDef once for the run (or pass through), and skip the push + gh pr create + run.vars._pr assignment when `def.auto_pr === false`. The auto-commit itself (git add + git commit) still runs — only the PR side-effects are gated.
- templates/workflows/daily-mutator.yaml — add top-level `auto_pr: false` (right after `name:`).
- templates/workflows/gepa-prep.yaml — add top-level `auto_pr: false` (right after `name:`).
- Leave templates/workflows/idea-to-pr.yaml, fix-bug.yaml, dynamic-feature.yaml untouched (default true).

Tests (write FIRST, watch them fail):
- tests/unit/workflow-parser.test.ts — extend with: (a) `auto_pr: true` round-trips, (b) `auto_pr: false` round-trips, (c) omitting `auto_pr` leaves the field undefined, (d) non-boolean `auto_pr` throws.
- tests/unit/workflow-auto-pr.test.ts (NEW) — verify the engine skips PR creation when auto_pr is false. Recommended approach: extract a small pure helper from applyAutoCommit such as `shouldCreatePr(def: WorkflowDef): boolean { return def.auto_pr !== false }` exported from src/workflow/engine.ts, and unit-test it directly. Cover: missing field => true, true => true, false => false. (Do NOT mock execSync or shell out from this test.)

Constraints:
- No `as any` / `as unknown as` casts.
- No Co-Authored-By trailers in commits.
- Keep all other workflow yaml files and engine call-sites unchanged.
- bun run typecheck must stay clean; bun test must stay green.

Note: another node in this DAG (mutator-fanout) also edits templates/workflows/daily-mutator.yaml in a different region (the `mutate` step body). Your edit should only add the top-level `auto_pr: false` line — leave the steps array alone — so the reconciler can three-way-merge cleanly.

flt workflow pass when tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.

Add workflow-scheduling support to `flt cron add`. Existing `flt cron add <agent-name> --every <ms>` must keep working unchanged. New shape: `flt cron add <workflow-name> <cron-spec>` schedules `flt workflow run <workflow-name>` on the given crontab spec.

Files to modify:
- src/commands/cron.ts — add a `cronAddWorkflow(name: string, cronSpec: string, opts: { task?: string; dir?: string; parent?: string }): void` (or extend cronAdd to dispatch when a positional cron-spec is provided). Validate the cron-spec is exactly 5 whitespace-separated fields (regex `/^\S+\s+\S+\s+\S+\s+\S+\s+\S+$/` is sufficient — do not parse the fields). Generate a script at ~/.flt/bin/cron-<name> whose body invokes `flt workflow run <name>` (with optional `--task`, `--dir`, `--parent` flags if those opts are supplied), then write the crontab line `<cron-spec> <scriptPath> >> <logPath> 2>&1` using the existing readCrontab/writeCrontab helpers. Reuse `parseCrontabLines` as-is — its current `cron-<name>` extraction already covers workflow entries. Refuse to install if a script for that name already exists (same precondition as agent cronAdd).
- src/cli.ts — change the `cronCmd.command('add <name>')` definition to `cronCmd.command('add <name> [cron-spec]')`. In the action: if `cron-spec` is provided, route to the new workflow path; otherwise require `--every` and route to existing `cronAdd`. Keep the existing `--send` / `--spawn` / `--every` flags wired to the agent path. Add a `--task <task>` flag (forwarded to `flt workflow run`).

Tests (write FIRST):
- tests/unit/cron.test.ts — extend with:
  (a) `cronAddWorkflow('daily-mutator', '0 3 * * *', {})` writes a script whose contents include `flt workflow run daily-mutator` (assert via readFileSync). Use `mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'flt-cron-'))` for HOME and stub `~/.flt/bin` / `~/.flt/logs` accordingly. DO NOT touch the real crontab — extract the script-generation step into a pure function (e.g. `generateWorkflowScript(name, opts)`) so the test never shells out to `crontab`.
  (b) cron-spec validation: an invalid spec like `'every 5 minutes'` throws.
  (c) `parseCrontabLines` correctly extracts a workflow entry of the form `0 3 * * * /Users/x/.flt/bin/cron-daily-mutator >> /Users/x/.flt/logs/cron-daily-mutator.log 2>&1` — schedule, name, and logPath fields populated.

Constraints:
- No `as any` / `as unknown as` casts.
- Keep `intervalToCron`, `cronToHuman`, `parseTimeout`, `cronPeriodMs`, `cronList`, `cronRemove`, and the agent-shape `cronAdd` exports backward-compatible — existing tests in tests/unit/cron.test.ts must stay green.
- bun run typecheck clean; bun test green.

flt workflow pass when tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.

Carve out ~/.flt/agents/<name>/ as a SAFE workdir in `isDangerousWorkdir`, and stop reading stdin from `confirmDangerousWorkdir` when running under the TUI. TDD: update tests first, watch them fail, then implement.

Files to modify:
- src/commands/spawn.ts — 
  (a) `isDangerousWorkdir(dir)` (around lines 67-85): keep ~/.flt itself dangerous, keep direct subtrees like ~/.flt/skills dangerous, but treat ~/.flt/agents/<name>/ (and any deeper path under a named agent dir) as SAFE. The bare ~/.flt/agents directory itself stays dangerous (spawning at the agents root would not be a per-agent home). Implementation hint: after the existing dot-config loop matches a candidate dir at ~/.flt, check whether the path is `~/.flt/agents/<name>` or deeper (length > the agents-prefix and a non-empty agent-name segment) and override to safe; otherwise return true as today. Keep the function pure and synchronous.
  (b) Export `isDangerousWorkdir` so the test can import it (eliminates the mirror-drift hazard called out in the existing test's header comment).
  (c) `confirmDangerousWorkdir(dir)` (around lines 87-99): when `process.env.FLT_TUI_ACTIVE === '1'`, do NOT create the readline interface or read stdin. Instead, write one stderr line like `flt: refusing to spawn into dangerous workdir under TUI: <dir>` and resolve to `false`. Otherwise behavior is unchanged.

- tests/unit/spawn-workdir-safety.test.ts — 
  (a) Replace the in-test mirrored `isDangerousWorkdir` with `import { isDangerousWorkdir } from '../../src/commands/spawn'`.
  (b) FLIP the existing assertion at line 59 (currently `expect(isDangerousWorkdir(join(FIXED_HOME, '.flt', 'agents', 'foo'))).toBe(true)`) to `false`, and rephrase the `it(...)` description to match.
  (c) Add new cases:
        - `~/.flt/agents/foo` => false (allowed)
        - `~/.flt/agents/foo/sub` => false (allowed, nested)
        - `~/.flt/agents` => true (still dangerous; bare agents root)
        - `~/.flt` => true (unchanged)
        - `~/.flt/skills` => true (unchanged)
        - HOME => true (unchanged)
  (d) Add a new `describe('confirmDangerousWorkdir under FLT_TUI_ACTIVE', ...)` block that sets `process.env.FLT_TUI_ACTIVE = '1'`, calls the function, asserts it resolves to `false` synchronously (no stdin read), and restores the env var in afterEach. Capture stderr (e.g. via spying on `process.stderr.write`) and assert one line was emitted that includes the dir.

Constraints:
- No `as any` / `as unknown as` casts.
- Do not touch the dangerous-workdir guard for ~/.claude, ~/.codex, ~/.opencode, ~/.gemini — only ~/.flt/agents/<name> gets the carve-out.
- bun run typecheck clean; bun test green.

flt workflow pass when tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.
